Dear doctor, clinical psychologist, psychotherapist, psychiatry resident, and student!

– The first systematic clinical psychiatry reference in Azerbaijani language. This book is designed for use in daily clinical practice by psychiatrist physicians, clinical psychologists, psychotherapists, family physicians, general practitioners, neurologists, and students studying in the field of psychiatry.

— Physician-psychiatrist and psychotherapist, clinical experience 23 years (since 2003). Graduated from Azerbaijan State Medical University in 2001, completed internship at the No. 2 Clinical Psychiatric Hospital in Baku the same year. During 2001–2003 in Saint Petersburg
At V.M. Bekhterev Psychoneurological Research Institute
Chief Psychotherapist of the Russian Federation, world-class expert Professor B.D. Karvasarski
under supervision in the psychotherapy department for neuroses and
Professor V.D. Vidin, one of the first certified psychoanalysts in the post-Soviet space, certified by the International Psychoanalytical Association (IPA).

under supervision in the psychotherapy department for psychoses. Over 23 years of clinical practice, more than 1000 patients with depressive disorders and more than 1000 patients with childhood nocturnal enuresis diagnosis have been treated; only in 2025, the author's clinical team successfully treated more than 250 children. Since 2020

founder, since 2021 authorized representative in Azerbaijan of the Karvasarsky Institute in St. Petersburg and head of the Additional Professional Education (APO) program in Clinical Psychology, since 2022 Ambassador of the International Psychotherapy Association (IPAS). Two author clinical protocols presented in this book —
treatment of nocturnal enuresis in children and two-stage treatment strategy for psychogenic depression

— Reflects modification of international evidence-based guidelines (NICE, APA, WFSBP) based on author's long-term clinical experience and retrospective clinical observations. These modifications allow achieving higher clinical effectiveness in shorter time compared to standard international protocols; evidence level IV (clinical expert experience) should be accepted. Author's recommendations stem from clinical expertise and 23 years of continuous practice; reader himself/herself can test, evaluate, and if necessary adapt these approaches in their own clinical practice.
